Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Person(S) Most Knowledgeable And Custodian(S) Of Records Of Intervenor American States Insurance Company (A Safeco Company) On Behalf Of Defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation , And To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James C. Parker of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for intervenor and moving party American States Insurance Company.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff and opposing party Robert Ross.

Parties stipulated to all discovery motions to be heard simultaneously.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and the Court rules Plaintiff's Motion to Compel the Deposition of Person(s) Most Knowledgeable and Custodian(s) of Records of Intervenor American States Insurance Company (a Safeco Company) on Behalf of Defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation, and to Compel Production of Documents is denied. Plaintiff notifies the court of a correction to the exhibits numbering in the Hixson case in relation to the other discovery motion. Court asserts Exhibit C of Notice of deposition is addressed to Aladdin Heating Corporation and not to Intervenor and therefore Aladdin Heating, suspended for failure to file taxes, cannot participate in litigation. Discovery was served to Intervenor and Intervenor responded. Notice not enforceable against Corporation. Court to issue order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537, email: early.langley@outlook.com (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff Robert Ross.

Court continued Plaintiff's Motion to Compel the Deposition of Person(s) Most Knowledgeable and Custodian(s) of Records of Intervenor American States Insurance Company (a Safeco Company) on Behalf of Defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation, and to Compel Production of Documents to Nov-19-2020 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 503 for moving party to provide a court reporter. Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen E. Carlson of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000), appearing for moving parties: specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. as alleged insurers of defendant Williams and Burrows Inc.

Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Robert Ross (274099), Michael Steinberger (274642), Willie Howard (275138), Monroe Amey (275535) and Joan Valladon as wrongful death heir and as successor-in-interest to George Valladon, deceased, et al. (421945).

RULING:
SPECIALLY APPEARING HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, NATIONWIDE INSURANCE CO.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST WILLIAMS AND BURROWS INC.

On the record the parties stipulated to argue all the motions in the following cases at the same hearing: Robert Ross (274099), Michael Steinberger (274642), Willie Howard (275138), Monroe Amey (275535) and George Valladon (421945).

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co.'s motion to set aside the default and default judgment entered against defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc. is granted in part and denied in part. Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. demonstrated that the Court should invoke its inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake because they demonstrated a meritorious defense, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action, and diligence in seeking to set aside the default judgment once it was discovered. Therefore the default judgment entered against Williams & Burrows, Inc. on December 10, 2015 is set aside solely as related to Hartford Fire Insurance Company and Nationwide Insurance Co. The motion to set aside the entry of default is denied. With the default judgment set aside, it is unnecessary to set aside the entry of default because an insured's default has no effect on the insurer(s). (Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Janice H. Dispo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13299 (Tel: 510-451-1580). (503/CML)

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied without prejudice and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (breach of duty) and granted as to issue 2 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that it did not know or should not reasonably have known that the asbestos-containing materials its employees exposed Mr. Ross to were hazardous. Defendant did not present any responses to contention interrogatories posed to plaintiff seeking this information. Plaintiff concedes that is punitive damages claim should be dismissed. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Melissa M. Corica, Esq. of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Fluor Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert Ross

RULING:
Defendant Fluor Corporation's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 2 (the Privette doctrine), taken off calendar as to issues 3-5 (strict products liability, false representation and punitive damages) since those matters have been dismissed, and denied as to issue 1 (exposure to asbestos-containing materials). There is a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant at the Union Oil Refinery between 1960 and 1966. Assuming without deciding that some variant of the sophisticated use/intermediary defense might potentially apply to the negligence and contractor liability claims against defendant, defendant has not established either the knowledge or constructive knowledge of either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of asbestos or the knowledge of defendant regarding what was known or should have been known by either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of asbestos, so any such defense has not been established as a matter of law. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
